setup.longrepr: ('/home/runner/work/my-osint-tools-dev/my-osint-tools-dev/tests/test_bookmarklets.py', 129, "Skipped: The bookmarklet requires a specific Google Maps 'contrib' review page where it can find span.rsqaWe (the review timestamp anchor) and an open review with a contributor ID + place ID + review ID embedded in APP_INITIALIZATION_STATE or reachable via the timeline _rpc/pc API. Google Maps review pages render dynamically, often require scrolling/expanding a review, and the exact DOM/class names are brittle and frequently change. This cannot be reliably reproduced headless in CI.")

('/home/runner/work/my-osint-tools-dev/my-osint-tools-dev/tests/test_bookmarklets.py', 129, "Skipped: The bookmarklet only works when a specific photo/video/360 lightbox is open and the page URL contains a '!6s(https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/...)' segment. This requires interactive navigation (clicking into the photo viewer) which isn't reproduced by simply loading target_url, and the bookmarklet opens the extracted image in a new tab rather than showing an in-page modal - neither behavior is reliably testable headless in CI.")

setup.longrepr: ('/home/runner/work/my-osint-tools-dev/my-osint-tools-dev/tests/test_bookmarklets.py', 129, "Skipped: Instagram post pages render their content (and the time[datetime] elements this bookmarklet scans for) via client-side JS that typically requires a logged-in session; in headless CI without authentication, Instagram shows a login wall and no time[datetime] elements are present, so the bookmarklet would only hit its 'No timestamp elements found on this page.' alert path. Requires auth that can't be worked around in CI.")

('/home/runner/work/my-osint-tools-dev/my-osint-tools-dev/tests/test_bookmarklets.py', 129, "Skipped: The bookmarklet requires a 'csrftoken' cookie (i.e. being logged in to Instagram) and immediately alerts 'Not logged in to Instagram' and returns if it's missing. It then calls Instagram's authenticated GraphQL endpoint (graphql/query with x-csrftoken/x-ig-app-id headers) to page through all of a user's posts. This cannot be exercised in unauthenticated headless CI.")

setup.longrepr: ('/home/runner/work/my-osint-tools-dev/my-osint-tools-dev/tests/test_bookmarklets.py', 129, 'Skipped: Relies on the page\'s meta[name="description"] containing a \' - <name> on Instagram:\' pattern and the raw document.body.innerHTML containing a \'profilePage_<id>"\' marker, both of which Instagram only renders for fully server-rendered/logged-out-compatible profile pages in certain locales/conditions. In current headless CI (no session, possible login wall or React-only shell), these markers are frequently absent, leaving displayName/username/instagramId as \'Not Found\'. The modal would still render with \'Not Found\' placeholders, which doesn\'t meaningfully validate extraction, so this is skipped pending a known-good fixture.')

('/home/runner/work/my-osint-tools-dev/my-osint-tools-dev/tests/test_bookmarklets.py', 129, "Skipped: Bookmarklet is meant to run on an individual Instagram post page (appends 'media/?size=l' to the current URL) and opens a new window/tab containing the raw image. Instagram media URLs require an authenticated session to load reliably, and the bookmarklet's effect (a new window) cannot be asserted in headless CI.")

setup.longrepr: ('/home/runner/work/my-osint-tools-dev/my-osint-tools-dev/tests/test_bookmarklets.py', 129, "Skipped: This bookmarklet works on the CURRENT page's URL, extracting a large numeric ID from the end of the path (e.g. a TikTok video/photo URL like https://www.tiktok.com/@user/video/1234567890123456789) and decoding the embedded timestamp from the first 31 bits of its binary representation. It requires a stable individual video/photo URL with a long numeric ID; we don't have a guaranteed-stable public video URL to pin in CI (TikTok videos can be deleted/removed at any time). On a plain profile page (no trailing large number) the bookmarklet just shows an alert ('Invalid URL...'), so testing against the profile URL would not exercise the real modal path.")

('/home/runner/work/my-osint-tools-dev/my-osint-tools-dev/tests/test_bookmarklets.py', 129, "Skipped: Tinder profile pages (https://tinder.com/@username or similar) require an active logged-in session to view any profile data - anonymous visitors are redirected to a login/marketing page with none of the expected page-source fields (id, username, birth_date, company, schools, gotinder.com photo URLs). The bookmarklet would either find nothing (all fields empty) or error out, neither of which meaningfully exercises the real modal. There's no stable public Tinder profile URL usable in CI.")

setup.longrepr: ('/home/runner/work/my-osint-tools-dev/my-osint-tools-dev/tests/test_bookmarklets.py', 129, "Skipped: The bookmarklet works by scanning inline <script> tags on the page for an already-embedded VK API 'users.get' JSON response containing a 'last_seen' field. This embedded API call is part of VK's server-rendered profile bootstrap data, which VK typically only includes for sessions with an authenticated/logged-in viewer (and may vary by locale/A-B test). For an anonymous visitor the expected script content is unlikely to be present, so the bookmarklet would just alert('Could not extract user data') rather than exercise the real modal. No reliable way to verify this works anonymously in CI.")

('/home/runner/work/my-osint-tools-dev/my-osint-tools-dev/tests/test_bookmarklets.py', 129, "Skipped: This bookmarklet's most data-rich path (identifier/X ID, account creation date, 'About this account' location/source data) relies on an authenticated GraphQL request (AboutAccountQuery) using a csrf token read from the 'ct0' cookie, which is only present for logged-in sessions. Without auth, the request either fails or returns nothing, and several fields (X ID, create date) are themselves usually only embedded in page scripts for logged-in views since X heavily restricts anonymous access to profile pages (often showing a login wall). No stable way to validate the full modal in anonymous CI.")
